title: Habenaria parishii
intro: Habenaria parishii, also known as the Parish's Habenaria, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and is valued for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria parishii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Habenaria parishii is commonly prepared as a decoction or an extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Habenaria parishii is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Habenaria and is a type of terrestrial orchid. The species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Further classification includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. Its classification as a flowering plant also places it within the subkingdom Angiospermahae.
